hypothesis: If language models for materials design are fine-tuned using physics-based preference optimization (DPO with thermodynamic simulation rewards) following supervised fine-tuning on known compositions, then they will generate BCC/B2 superalloy candidates with higher thermodynamic validity, greater novelty, and broader compositional coverage compared to supervised fine-tuning alone, few-shot prompting of large API models, and agentic baselines, because aligning model outputs to domain-specific physical objectives directly shapes the generative distribution toward chemically and thermodynamically feasible regions of composition space.
